title: India’s homegrown answer to S-400? Rajnath says Project Kusha is a game changer
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh hailed indigenous Project Kusha as a "game changer" for India's security, comparing its protective role to the mythical Govardhan Hill. This advanced, long-range surface-to-air missile system, designed to counter diverse aerial threats, proved its mettle during Operation Sindoor. Singh emphasized its crucial role in bolstering India's defence capabilities amidst a volatile global landscape.

title: Tu cheez badi hain....Musk Musk: A small step for SpaceX, a giant leap to a trillion for Elon
content: Elon Musk has achieved a historic milestone by becoming the world’s first trillionaire, driven by the soaring valuation from SpaceX’s IPO. His vision for reusable rockets and interplanetary colonies has captured the imagination of investors, turning space exploration into an enticing financial frontier. This remarkable shift in wealth underscores a new chapter in capitalism, expanding its reach beyond conventional sectors.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's 10-year-old younger brother Aashirwad slams century
content: Younger brother Aashirwad Sooryavanshi, aged 10, has scored a brilliant century in a local practice match, mirroring the cricketing talent of his elder brother Vaibhav. Vaibhav, a teenage sensation, proudly shared Aashirwad's achievement on social media while he is currently on tour with India A.

title: 'Give ourselves options': Morkel outlines India's fast-bowling vision for 2027 ODI World Cup
content: India's fast-bowling reserves are being tested in the Afghanistan series as part of a long-term strategy for the South Africa tour. Bowling coach Morne Morkel highlighted the importance of giving youngsters like Prince Yadav and Gurnoor Brar opportunities to develop under pressure, aiming for peak performance in next year's World Cup.

title: How Sergey Brin, Larry Ellison & others are turning Nevada town into ‘billionaire bunker’
content: Wealthy tech leaders are moving to Incline Village, Nevada. This shift is driven by California's proposed 2026 Billionaire Tax Act. Nevada offers a tax-friendly environment with no income tax. This move aims to secure financial advantages. Prominent figures like Sergey Brin and Larry Ellison are reportedly settling in the area. Real estate sales have significantly increased.

title: Planning of cities must be people-centric, says Khattar as Brics forum adopts urban pact
content: Cities must prioritize people and their needs, according to Housing Minister Manohar Lal, as BRICS nations adopted a declaration for inclusive urban development. The 'Cities for People' forum stressed equitable access to housing and services, with India facing significant future urbanization. Strengthening local governments and embracing digital solutions were also key takeaways for creating sustainable, liveable urban centers.

title: Declassified US records reveal UFO sightings over Ladakh, Sikkim in 1968 - what files show
content: Declassified US government UFO files reveal multiple aerial sightings over India and Nepal in February and March 1968. Reports detail objects emitting light and sound, with one incident in Nepal leading to the discovery of a metallic disc-shaped object in a crater. These disclosures are part of a broader UAP records release.

title: Google donates $50mn to train 300K workers, CEO says infrastructure is key
content: Google is investing $50 million through Google.org to train 300,000 American workers for skilled trade jobs like electricians, welders, and plumbers. This initiative aims to bolster the nation's digital economy by supporting the physical infrastructure built and maintained by these essential workers. The funding will empower various labor unions and trade associations across more than 20 states.

title: Amarnath Yatra: Shah orders multi-layered security, pushes tech-driven pilgrim safety
content: Home Minister Amit Shah has ordered a robust, multi-layered security grid for the Amarnath Yatra, utilizing advanced technology like drones and CCTV. He stressed securing tourist spots for safe pilgrimage and tourism. The 57-day yatra, starting July 3, will see strict regulation of pilgrim movement and enhanced essential services, with QR code IDs for local support staff and animals.

title: YouTuber ‘Masood Chandi’ murdered: Bodybuilder bludgeoned to death with rods in Hyderabad
content: A 25-year-old bodybuilder and YouTuber, known as Masood Chandi, was fatally attacked at his Hyderabad home by his brother-in-law and associates. The brutal assault, stemming from personal disputes and marital issues, resulted in the victim's death from severe head injuries. Police have launched a manhunt for the accused.

title: 64 of 80 TMC MLAs turn rebel? Trouble mounts for Mamata as Ritabrata ready for floor test
content: West Bengal's leader of opposition Ritabrata Banerjee, claimed the backing of 64 Trinamool Congress MLAs, exceeding a two-thirds majority. He stated this list was submitted to the Speaker, who could call for a floor test. Banerjee also hinted at more than 20 Lok Sabha MPs potentially aligning with the rebellion, echoing long-standing concerns within the party.

title: SpaceX makes Musk world’s 1st trillionaire: Will his net worth sustain?
content: It’s important to note that a big portion of Musk’s net worth is tied to SpaceX. His holding in the company is worth around $866 billion. According to Reuters calculations, Musk’s net worth adds up to over a trillion dollars after the listing of Space X.

title: Over 200 trips to the moon and more : What Elon Musk's trillion could mean
content: Elon Musk's potential trillion-dollar fortune is a monumental sum, dwarfing national economies and astronomical distances.  This unprecedented wealth, if realized, would be enough to buy millions of homes or fuel the entire US for over a year, highlighting a growing concern over global wealth inequality.

title: Taylor Swift’s songwriting triumph turns viral as Travis Kelce steals scene
content: Taylor Swift handed Steven Spielberg her phone during her Songwriters Hall of Fame celebration, and Travis Kelce joined the viral dance moment in New York.

title: Hurricanes’ Bobrovsky pursuit collapses as Panthers Demand too much
content: The Carolina Hurricanes were discussing the acquisition of Florida Panthers goalie Sergei Bobrovsky before the 2026 NHL trade deadline. But differences over compensation prevented a move. Carolina is chasing the Stanley Cup, and Bobrovsky’s future in Florida is up in the air, so the reported talks have become one of the latest NHL trade rumours drawing attention.

title: Brandon Bussi’s family shared an emotional reaction after Game 5 triumph
content: The Carolina Hurricanes are a win from the Stanley Cup after beating the Vegas Golden Knights 4-2 in Game 5. Andrei Svechnikov was the offensive star, but one of the emotional highlights of the night came after the final buzzer when Brandon Bussi’s parents were spotted celebrating in the stands after another steady performance by the young goaltender.

title: 'Stand-up comedy shows should be banned': Mumbai mayor Ritu Tawde
content: Mumbai Mayor Ritu Tawde has called for a ban on stand-up comedy shows following a controversy involving comedian Pranit More. This comes after an FIR was registered for allegedly obscene content from a show, sparking debate on freedom of expression versus societal dignity.

title: Jaishankar exposes EU double-standard: The weapons Pakistan got from Europe
content: S Jaishankar, the External Affairs Minister of India, has expressed his frustration with what he perceives as Europe's double standards regarding energy dealings with Russia. He noted that European nations, while admonishing India, have a troubling record of arming countries that have actively threatened India, notably strengthening Pakistan’s defense for decades.

title: Outspoken, controversial: Jaspal Rana was Indian shooting's original prodigy
content: Indian shooting legend Jaspal Rana, known for his fearless approach and straight talk, has passed away at 49. A trailblazer, he inspired a generation of shooters, proving Indians could win internationally. Rana challenged the system, championed athletes' rights, and later mentored champions like Manu Bhaker, leaving an indelible mark on the sport.

title: 'From an opponent to a friend': Kohli pens heartfelt farewell message for Kane
content: Virat Kohli penned an emotional tribute to Kane Williamson following the New Zealand great's retirement from international cricket. Kohli lauded their years of competition and cherished their friendship, wishing Williamson well in his post-cricket life. Williamson concluded a stellar 16-year career, leaving a significant legacy as one of New Zealand's finest cricketers.

title: 'Repeatedly humiliated me': Pune techie at TCS hangs himself; FIR registered against two
content: A Pune IT firm employee's alleged suicide has led to a case of abetment against three individuals, including two TCS colleagues. The deceased's son filed a complaint, citing a suicide note that accused a friend of sending defamatory emails and two female colleagues of humiliation, denial of assignments, and threats. Police are investigating.

title: Can India become a mango export powerhouse? Opportunities, challenges and road ahead
content: Despite being the world's largest mango producer, India exports only a very small fraction of its output due. While "mango diplomacy" and processed products show promise, overcoming challenges in cold chains, treatment facilities, and traceability is crucial for India to achieve export leadership. Logistics gap, infrastructure were also cited by exporters.

title: Can builders sell parking space to homebuyers seprately? What RERA actually allows
content: RERA rules clarify homebuyer parking rights. Open parking is a common area, not for sale by developers. Garages, with three walls and a roof, can be sold separately. Developers must declare parking details, including size and location, in allotment letters and approved plans, ensuring transparency and reducing disputes.

title: Nora Fatehi to headline FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony in Canada
content: Canada will host the second FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony in Toronto. Nora Fatehi will headline the event. Other artists like Alanis Morissette and Michael Buble will also perform. The ceremony precedes Canada's first match against Bosnia and Herzegovina. Fans can watch the event live on UNITE8 Sports and DD Sports. Live streaming is available on Zee5.

title: Sanctions relief, Lebanon ceasefire inclusion: Inside 14-point US-Iran deal proposal
content: Reports indicate the US and Iran are advancing a 14-point draft understanding to end hostilities and ease sanctions, potentially paving the way for a nuclear deal. The framework includes a comprehensive ceasefire, sanctions suspension, and a $300 billion reconstruction commitment, with a 60-day negotiation period to finalize a settlement.

title: WBJEE answer key 2026 released: Direct link to raise challenges
content: The West Bengal Joint Entrance Examination Board (WBJEEB) has released WBJEE 2026 response sheets and OMR images for candidates. Students can log in to view machine-read answers and verify records. The board has also opened a limited window to raise challenges against discrepancies, with strict deadlines and a ₹500 fee per response review.

title: Lifeline for India's heavy-ift fleet? US weighs C-17 production restart
content: US lawmakers are exploring restarting C-17 Globemaster III production due to rising operational demands, a move keenly watched by India. The Indian Air Force, which desired more C-17s before production ceased, still faces a significant heavy-lift gap. Boeing is open to restarting, but challenges remain, potentially offering India a chance to bolster its strategic airlift capabilities.

title: Synergy of steel & software: How Indian Railways got on the digital track
content: Indian Railways is undergoing a massive digital overhaul, transforming the passenger and freight experience. From booking tickets on smartphones to real-time train tracking and advanced safety systems like Kavach, technology is now central. This digital shift, powered by data and AI, aims to create a more efficient and connected transportation network, though challenges like cybersecurity and digital literacy remain.

title: 'Sole purpose not to apportion blame': AAIB on probe progress in Air India crash
content: One year after the Air India Flight AI-171 crash, the AAIB issued an interim statement, expressing condolences and assuring a comprehensive investigation focused on safety enhancements, not blame. The probe has made significant progress in analyzing technical, operational, and human factors. The Civil Aviation Minister reiterated commitment to a thorough and objective determination of the accident's causes.

title: How women's T20s got bigger, faster and bolder
content: On the eve of the Women's T20 World Cup, a deep dive into how women's cricket quietly underwent its own batting revolution. From Charlotte Edwards and Suzie Bates to Beth Mooney, Hayley Matthews and Shafali Verma, the game has shifted from preserving wickets to exploiting powerplays. Run rates among elite nations are up 27%, boundaries have surged and franchise leagues have changed batting habits, making 150 the new benchmark.

title: Kick Streamer reveals real reason why he is distancing himself from UFC Freedom 250
content: Adin Ross has revealed that he will not attend UFC Freedom 250 at the White House this weekend. The popular streamer shared his reasons during a recent live broadcast. His comments surprised many fans because Ross previously supported Donald Trump and even hosted the current U.S. president on one of the most-watched streams of his career. However, Ross now says he wants to avoid political involvement and focus on other projects.

title: More trouble for Mamata: FIR against ex-Bengal CM over 'inflammatory' speech
content: An FIR has been filed against former West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee for alleged provocative remarks made at a Kolkata rally. The complaint cites concerns over public peace and communal harmony. The TMC has labelled the case politically motivated amidst the party's internal dissent and recent election results, with investigations now underway.

title: Explained: Why Virat Kohli is not playing the first ODI vs Afghanistan in Dharamsala
content: Team India starts its 2027 ODI World Cup journey against Afghanistan. Shubman Gill leads the squad in Dharamsala. Virat Kohli is out with a hamstring injury. Kohli has been in strong ODI form since 2025. India plays 20 ODIs before the World Cup. The team hopes for Kohli's quick recovery for the England tour.

title: Dhamaal 4 trailer out: Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi return with more chaos
content: The much-awaited Dhamaal 4 trailer is finally out, launched at Imagicaa in Mumbai. Packed with fun, adventure and chaos, it captures the trademark energy of the beloved franchise. The film stars Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi, Riteish Deshmukh, Jaaved Jaaferi and Sanjay Mishra, and is directed by Indra Kumar.

title: 'Jaspal Rana was heading to Germany when ... ': Uncle reveals final details
content: Legendary Indian shooter and coach Jaspal Rana has passed away. He was a celebrated athlete, achieving numerous medals at the Commonwealth and Asian Games. Rana also excelled at the World Championships. He later mentored young shooters as a high-performance coach. His contributions to Indian sports are significant.

title: 'Abortion pills, pool of blood': Nursing UP student found dead, boyfriend nabbed
content: A 20-year-old nursing student was found dead on guest house stairs, suspected to have died from medication taken to abort her three-month pregnancy. Her boyfriend, a fellow student, confessed to administering the drug after doctors refused to perform the procedure without parental consent. He claims he panicked and fled when she began bleeding heavily.

title: 'On my own terms': Kane Williamson retires from international cricket
content: New Zealand's star batsman Kane Williamson has retired from international cricket with immediate effect after a distinguished 16-year career. The batting stalwart, who represented New Zealand in 378 matches across all formats, stated the decision was not sudden and he felt it was the right time to step away on his own terms, expressing optimism for the team's future.

title: Abhishek Banerjee Gets CID Notice In Speech Case, Asked To Appear On Tuesday
content: A team from the West Bengal CID reached Banerjee's residence in the afternoon to serve the notice. However, the Diamond Harbour MP was not present at home when the officers arrived.

title: At Municipal Poll Win Rally, Arvind Kejriwal Seeks Support For Bhagwant Mann 2.0
content: Arvind Kejriwal unequivocally positioned Bhagwant Mann as the AAP's chief ministerial candidate in Punjab for a second term, highlighting his "clean image" and governance record

title: Tulsi Gabbard Reveals US Funding For 120 Biolabs In Over 30 Countries
content: The Office of the Director of National Intelligence statement indicated that many of these facilities have engaged in research involving hazardous and highly contagious pathogens, including "Gain-of-Function" research, with what the DNI described as minimal oversight.

title: El Nino Raises Ocean Temperatures: How Marine Life Could Be Affected
content: With an El Nino expected to strengthen through the summer and fall, water temperatures will heat up even more. It's time to start preparing.

title: 'Should Ban Stand-up Comedy Shows': Mumbai Mayor After Cadaver, Biryani Rows
content: Ritu Tawde's remarks on Friday came a day after Maharashtra Cyber registered an FIR over allegedly obscene and objectionable content circulated online from a comedy show of Pranit More.

title: Mediator Pakistan Says Iran-US Peace Deal Text 'Agreed'
content: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi insisted on Friday that a deal with the United States to end the war in the Middle East had never been closer.

title: 'Give Us PoK': J&K Border Villagers Hold Prayers On 12 Years Of Modi Government
content: "No more we have to abandon our homes because of shells fired by trigger-happy Pakistani soldiers," says Garu, a resident of a border village

title: Trump, Netanyahu Started Iran War, Their Political Goals Are Now Causing Rift
content: Trump is looking ahead to the US midterm elections in November and wants to bring the unpopular Iran war to an end. Netanyahu, who also faces elections this year, has a different challenge.

title: Pune TCS Engineer Dies By Suicide, Blames Seniors, Including 2 Women
content: TCS employee Amit Abhay Brahme died by suicide at his house on June 2. During the investigation, the police found a two-page note from his room

title: Bhopal Man Arrested, Was Allegedly Planning Lone-Wolf Attacks
content: The accused, identified as Mohd Faraz, is a resident of the Qazi Camp area of Old Bhopal. He was arrested by the ATS near Nanhe Bee's Mosque in a highly confidential operation.

title: 1 Dead, 9 Injured In Texas Shooting
content: Video from Mendias showed a number of officers pouring out of the back of an armoured police vehicle. Police were also sending robots into the area.

title: Expert Explains How Tepid US Response Aided Ebola Spread In Congo
content: Dr Ashish Jha blamed the Donald Trump administration's funding cuts for a muted global response to the outbreak in DR Congo.

title: Trump Claims Iran Tried To Attack Indian Ships With Drones, Slams Deal 'Leak'
content: US President Donald Trump's comment comes after three vessels with Indian crew came under attack off the Omani coast this week.

title: 'Worst May Be Behind Us': Chief Economic Adviser On US-Iran Conflict's Effects
content: NDTV Ignite Summit: "High frequency indicators have shown that domestic demand is holding up very well, and that would naturally mean better revenues for companies," Chief Economic Adviser V Anantha Nageswaran said

title: Chennai To Name Road After Ashoka Chakra Awardee Major Mukund Varadharajan
content: The Tambaram City Municipal Corporation Council has passed a resolution to rename the nearly 15-kilometre stretch connecting East Tambaram and Velachery as "Major Mukund Varadharajan Road."

title: International Labour Organisation Adopts First Treaty On Gig Workers' Safety
content: The Decent Work in the Platform Economy Convention is aimed at extending labour protections to hundreds of millions of people worldwide who work through digital platforms, in areas like food delivery and car services.

title: Delhi EV Policy Nears Finish Line, Hybrid Incentive A Sticking Point
content: Sources told NDTV that a proposal to offer a 50 per cent waiver on road tax and registration fees for strong hybrid vehicles remains under consideration and is likely to feature in the final version.

title: ITI management feud turns deadly in Bapatla district
content: Long-running dispute over the administration of an institute at Epurupalem village leaves one person dead and another critically injured

title: AIADMK plans to hold extensive, district-wise consultations shortly
content: Such an exercise will be helpful for the party, which has to face not only byelections to five Assembly constituencies but also local bodies’ polls in eight months, says a researcher of the party

title: Gold theft on Guruvayur-Chennai train: eight-member inter-state gang arrested
content: Railway Protection Force, in coordination with Tamil Nadu Government Railway Police, recover stolen property worth ₹2.5 crore

title: ‘Deep-seated’ bias for male child persists despite improving sex ratio, says Supreme Court
content: The top court refused to quash criminal proceedings against a Maharashtra doctor under the Pre-Conception and Pre-Natal Diagnostic Techniques (Prohibition of Sex Selection) Act, 1994

title: BRICS nations adopt resolution on people-centric urban development
content: The Ministerial declaration adopted on the concluding day of the two-day 13th BRICS Urbanisation Forum highlights key priorities shared by member nations and reaffirms commitment to advancing people-centred urban development

title: Attacks on ships ‘unacceptable’, says India; Trump blames Iran
content: Three ships attacked by U.S. had strong Indian links going beyond crew onboard; External Affairs Ministry summons Charge d’Affaires of U.S. Embassy Jason Meeks and lodges ‘strong protest’ for second time

title: Supported by CJP, Lucknow students protest lack of transparency, irregularities in recruitment drives
content: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke joins protest; protesters demand independent high-level inquiry into Lekhpal exam irregularities, seek permanent grievance redressal mechanism for competitive exam aspirants

title: One year on, families of Air India crash victims demand answers, accountability
content: Family members and the Federation of Indian Pilots bemoaned that there was no statement from the Prime Minister or the Gujarat Chief Minister, nor any public representative was present at the gathering

title: Kendriya Vidyalayas must have at least one Sanskrit section for Classes 6 and 9: KVS
content: Keeping Sanskrit as an option in all KVs ensures a smooth transition for students facing frequent transfers, letting others choose regional languages; some KVs face challenges of limited teachers and resources

title: ED attack case accused taken to LoP’s residence for evidence collection
content: Former Thiruvananthapuram Corporation councillor I.P. Binu was among the seven CPI(M) workers taken for evidence collection

title: Country is drifting towards electoral autocracy: Congress
content: Congress accuses BJP of subverting democratic institutions for political gain in connivance with the poll body and says the fight is between the ‘constitutional model’ and the ‘Modi model’.

title: Around 4,000 EVMs gutted as Bengal govt. building catches fire; Minister flags possibility of foul play
content: West Bengal Fire and Emergency Services Minister of State, Koushik Chowdhury, who went to visit the site, also flagged a possibility of sabotage

title: Govt. seeks public feedback on draft telecom rules on TV, radio services
content: In the draft rules, existing terms and conditions of authorisation have been harmonised and rationalised to facilitate continuity, while enabling reforms and provision for a single regulatory framework in place of multiple guidelines

title: Centre tells Supreme Court it is mulling policy for West Asia students whose CBSE results could not be declared
content: A student from Saudi Arabia whose results were withheld tells court that his higher education plans are now in jeopardy; court defers hearing to June 22

title: West Asia conflict: Andhra Pradesh seafarers caught in the line of fire
content: For thousands of Indian seafarers stranded in the Persian Gulf, the Strait of Hormuz has become less a shipping lane and more a trap.

title: FIR against former CM Mamata Banerjee over alleged communal remarks in Bengal
content: During a protest against the SIR, the TMC chief allegedly told a minority community that their safety depended on her, advising them not to pay heed to the BJP’s ‘misleading’ words

title: Seven migrant workers held captive by alleged job racket in Kochi rescued
content: The police arrested a West Bengal native, Abdul Rahim, in connection with the incident, while at least 11 others allegedly involved in the racket are reportedly on the run

title: BSF, BGB discuss illegal and forced border crossings at DG-level talks
content: India-Bangladesh border forces review illegal crossings, border security, and trans-border crime during four-day coordination conference in Delhi

title: Lt. Gen. Rajiv Ghai appointed Military Adviser to NSCS
content: For the first time, a serving military officer has been appointed Military Adviser to the NSCS, which functions under National Security Adviser Ajit Doval.

title: Home Minister Amit Shah reviews Amarnath Yatra security
content: He directed the officials to ensure robust security arrangements at other major tourist destinations besides the Yatra route; this year’s Yatra is scheduled from July 3 to August 28

title: AI-171 crash: AAIB releases interim statement on probe
content: Final report to be released after all investigative work has been completed, and the mandatory international review and consultation processes concluded, says Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau

title: Farmers and environmentalists oppose proposal for hydrocarbon wells off Parangipettai coast
content: They warn the mangroves will be under threat if the project is implemented, and it will destroy coastal natural resources and severely affect the livelihood of fishermen

title: Uttam Kumar Reddy meets Chhattisgarh CM, pursues NOC for Sammakka-Sarakka Project
content: Telangana Irrigation Minister says a joint survey is underway to identify areas of submergence in backwaters in Chhattisgarh

title: Watch: Trump slams Iran over peace deal leaks| Above the Fold| 12.06.2026
content: Could West Asia be heading towards a diplomatic breakthrough? In this episode, we unpack the reported draft agreement between Iran and the U.S. and what it could mean for the future of the conflict. We also look at the Supreme Court’s dismissal of Meenakshi Natarajan’s plea over her rejected Rajya Sabha nomination, and more.

title: Three students injured as ceiling plaster collapses in PU college
content: More than 80 were in the classroom triggering panic among students of the Government Pre-University College for Girls in Kalaburagi

title: Kochi Corporation to kick start drive to relocate authorised street vendors
content: Authorised vendors will be relocated in July to 67 dedicated vending zones identified across 76 divisions under the Street Vendors Act, 2014

title: New liquor policy after Budget: Liju
content: Excise Minister says discussions will take place within  UDF as well as with all stakeholders. The objective is to formulate a policy that is beneficial to Kerala and helps guide society forward

title: Horticulture Dept. to introduce 617 new species in Cubbon and Lalbagh, including endangered ones
content: While the department is introducing 617 saplings in Cubbon and Lalbagh from various institutions, the main focus is on bringing endangered species found in BR hills and conserved by the Soliga trive

title: BSL-3 lab in Kozhikode set to strengthen Nipah virus detection
content: The high-containment facility is expected to bolster the State’s diagnostic self-reliance and facilitate the early detection of high-consequence pathogens such as the Nipah virus

title: NHAI drops plan for barrier-free tolling on Bengaluru-Mysuru highway
content: The Bengaluru–Mysuru Access-Controlled Highway (NH-275) had earlier been identified as one of the corridors where NHAI planned to pilot the MLFF system.

title: Mallikarjun Kharge receives Rajya Sabha election certificate, says it might be his last term in RS
content: AICC president Mallikarjun Kharge received his certificate of election after being elected unopposed to the Rajya Sabha from Karnataka for a second consecutive term

title: Meet Antonio Gracias, Elon Musk’s Indian-origin friend set for billionaire status after SpaceX IPO
content: the 55-year-old founder of Valor Equity Partners has spent more than two decades supporting Musk’s ventures . he is set to make $68 billion, around Rs 6,468.16 crore, after the much-awaited IPO . his father, immigrated to the united states from Goa and eventually became a practising neurosurgeon in Detroit .

title: 'I don't wear a watch': Nvidia CEO reveals the best career advice he ever received
content: tycoon Jensen Huang, 63, is the CEO of Nvidia . he started his career while clearing tables and scrubbing floors at Denny’s . the best career advice he had ever received was from a gardener .

title: Trump blames Iran for attacking Indian ships amid New Delhi fury over death of 3 sailors
content: alleged drone attacks against Indian ships in the Strait of Hormuz are totally unACCEPTABLE . the attack comes amid growing anger in new Delhi over american missile strikes . mea lodges protest against attacks with Indians on board US Navy and Centcom forces .
